description.p: Crusaders are the embodiment of honour and virtue in martial combat. All across the land, scattered homes of crusader knights train for the betterment of the communities in which they serve. They stand tall as paragons of justice, beacons of strength and they have more than earned that reputation.

Crusaders utilise the strength and rigidness of armor and shield and the power of sword or maul to defend civilisation against the vile and vicious monsters known to infest the lands. Most crusaders come from so-called Crusader Clans, small outposts lead by a battlemaster who teach students how to defend others and when to push their advantage to slay the enemies of civilisation.

Each crusader clan has their own techniques and tactics, some relying on more offensive techniques and taking the fight directly to their enemies, others combining their defensive fighting with magic to create a shield against both martial and magical dangers. Despite their differences, the one thing that joins all crusaders is the desire to fight and protect all under their care.

tr.td: Choose 2: Athletics, History, Insight, Intimidation, Investigation, Medicine, Perception, Religion or Survival
Light, Medium and Heavy armor and Shields
Choose A, B: (A) Longsword, Javelin, Chain Mail and Shield or (B) 110 GP

ul.li: Gain all the traits in the Core Crusader Traits table.
Gain the Crusader's level 1 features, which are listed in the Crusader Features table.
Gain the following traits from the Core Crusader Traits table: Hit Point Die, proficiency with Martial weapons, and training with Light and Medium armor and Shields.
Gain the Crusader's level 1 features, which are listed in the Crusader Features table.

description.p: As a Crusader, you gain the following class features when you reach the specified Crusader levels. These features are listed in the Crusader Features table.

setters.set: A Warrior of Honour and Glory
Martial weapons, Light armor, Medium armor, Shields

description.p: You have a wellspring of resilience that you can call on in times of need. When you take damage, you can choose to reduce the damage you take by an amount equal to 1d10 + your Crusader level.

You can use this feature twice. You regain one expended use on a Short Rest and all expended uses on a Long Rest. You can use this feature three times starting at 4th level and four times starting at 10th level

sheet.description: When you take damage, you can choose to reduce the damage you take by 1d10+{{level:crusader}} HP. You regain one expended use when you finish a Short Rest.

description.p: Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of two kinds of Simple or Martial weapons of your choice. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can practice weapon drills and change one of those weapon choices.

sheet.description: Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change one of the weapons you previously chosen for a different one.

description.p: Starting at 2nd level, your training as a Crusader has allowed you to learn a series of special techniques that can be used to protect your allies and improve your offensive capabilities. As a bonus action, you activate this ward for the next minute or until you are Incapacitated. While this ward is active, you and allies within 10 feet of you cannot be Frightened. In addition, damage dealt to allies within 10 feet of you is halved.
You can use this feature twice. You regain one expended use at the end of a Short Rest and all expended uses at the end of a Long Rest.

At 9th level, the range of the aura is increased to 20 feet and you gain an additional usage of this feature. At 18th level, the range of the aura is increased to 30 feet and you gain an additional usage of this feature.

sheet.description: You activate this ward for the next minute or until you are Incapacitated. While this ward is active, you and allies within {{crusader ward:range}} feet of you cannot be Frightened. In addition, damage dealt to allies within {{crusader ward:range}} feet of you is halved.

description.p: You have honed your martial prowess and gain a Fighting Style feat of your choice. Defense is recommended.
Whenever you gain a Crusader level, you can replace the feat you chose with a different Fighting Style feat.

You gain a Crusader subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Crusader levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Crusader level or lower.
You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Crusader levels 8, 10, 12 and 16.

By 5th level, you have learnt how to inspire courage into your allies during dire times. As an action, you make a loud battle cry to all allied creatures within 60 feet that can hear you. Creatures that hear your battle cry gain temporary hit points equal to 1d12 plus your crusader level and gain a Heroic Inspiration if they lack it.
Once you have used this feature, you cannot use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

sheet.description: You make a loud battle cry to all allied creatures within 60 feet that can hear you. Creatures that hear your battle cry gain temporary hit points equal to 1d12+{{crusader:level}} and gain a Heroic Inspiration if they lack it.

description.p: Starting at 5th level, your Crusader training allows you to strike a decisive blow against your foes. When you make an attack with a melee weapon that you are proficient with, you can add 2d8 damage of your weapon’s damage type to the damage rolls. You can only add this extra damage once per turn.
The damage is increased to 3d8 at 9th level, 4d8 at 13th level and 5d8 at 17th level.

sheet.description: When you make an attack with a melee weapon that you are proficient with, you can add {{weak point:damage}} damage of your weapon’s damage type to the damage rolls. You can only add this extra damage once per turn.

description.p: When you activate your Crusader Ward, you and all allies within its range gain a +2 bonus to their AC. In addition, once per round when an ally fails a saving throw while within 10 feet of you, you can make them reroll. They must use the new result.

sheet.description: When you activate your Crusader Ward, you and all allies within its range gain a +2 bonus to their AC. In addition, once per round when an ally fails a saving throw while within 10 feet of you, you can make them reroll. They must use the new result.

description.p: Choose two saving throws. You gain proficiency if you do not already have it. If you already have proficiency in the saving throw you choose, you instead double your proficiency bonus for those saving throws.

You put your heart into each and every strike. You can add a bonus to damage rolls made with weapons with which you are proficient equal to your Constitution modifier. In addition, when you make an attack of opportunity, you can take two attacks instead of one.

sheet.description: You can add {{modifier:constitution}} extra damage to rolls made with weapons with which you are proficient with. In addition, when you make an attack of opportunity, you can take two attacks instead of one.

element.name: Level 14: Juggernaut

description.p: You are unstoppable while in combat. You reduce all damage you take by an amount equal to your Constitution modifier. In addition, any damage you deal with a weapon that you are proficient with deals additional damage equal to your Constitution modifier.

element.description: You reduce all damage you take by {{modifier:constitution}}. In addition, any damage you deal with a weapon that you are proficient with deals an additional {{modifier:constitution}} damage.

description.p: You can dig deep when times grow tough. While you are under half of your maximum hp, all attacks you make with weapons that you are proficient with are made at advantage.

sheet.description: While you are under half of your maximum hp, all attacks you make with weapons that you are proficient with are made at advantage.

description.p: You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

Upon reaching 20th level, you have become a paragon for defending and protecting your allies and you are able to resist even the forces of death for a time. As a bonus action, you can choose to prepare yourself against attack. For the next minute, you gain resistance to all incoming damage that you take. In addition, if you are reduced to 0 hit points while this is active, you are instead on 1 hit point and conscious. Once you use this feature, you must complete a long rest before you use it again.

sheet.description: You can choose to prepare yourself against attack. For the next minute, you gain resistance to all incoming damage that you take. In addition, if you are reduced to 0 hit points while this is active, you are instead on 1 hit point and conscious.
